editor_note: This reads as genuine, well-edited Arabic technical prose, not machine translation. Sentences are properly verb-initial and idiomatic (e.g. "تمتلك دالة generate_preset_pass_manager وسيطة موضعية مطلوبة واحدة", "يوضح الجدول التالي عمليات التحسين") rather than calqued English word order, and the teaching flow matches the English intuition step by step. Terminology is correct and internally consistent: "transpiler/transpile" is rendered المحوّل / التحويل throughout; "unitary operator" as المؤثر الوحدوي; "two-qubit gates" as بوابات الـ qubit الثنائية; qubit, backend, ECR, SWAP, and the pass names kept in Latin script as the field does. A notable sign of care: the English distinguishes "compile times" (line 35) from transpilation, and the translator preserved that with أوقات تصريف rather than collapsing it into التحويل. Semantics are faithful at the load-bearing spots — "not always make a difference" → "لا يُحدث فرقاً في كل الأحوال", "at most three ECR gates" → "ثلاث بوابات ECR كحد أقصى", and "active qubits, of which there are two" → "الـ qubits النشطة، وعددها اثنان" are all exact. "fidelity" is glossed as الإخلاص (fidelity), which is clear. Informal register (يمكنك، تجربته) is used appropriately with no formal antum slips. No drift, no inconsistency, nothing misleading.

This reads as genuine native technical-business Arabic, not machine output: idiomatic choices like "شُح الأشخاص ذوي الخبرة" (shortage of experienced people), "رهانات آمنة جدًا" (very safe bets), "يواكب آخر التطورات" (keeps abreast of developments), and "تجدر الإشارة إلى أنه" carry the same tone as the English, and careful diacritics (مُطَّلِعة، يُقسِّم، شُح) show real editing. Domain terms are correct and consistent: الحوسبة الكمية / أجهزة الكم throughout, "القطرنة" for diagonalization (lines 124/134/138, the project's canonical fix), "هاميلتوني" for Hamiltonian, "متراجحة CHSH" for CHSH inequality, and Qubit kept in Latin script consistently (lines 122, 160). Meaning is faithful end to end — no inverted negations, swapped FTE figures, or dropped caveats; the informal "anta" register holds (تُيسِّر، تساعدك، مؤسستك، خدماتك). Only two small non-misleading nits: "بطل كمي داخلي" for "internal quantum champion" (line 20) is a literal calque where the role is really an advocate, and "مشغِّلات Pauli المتبادلة" (line 126) uses المتبادلة ("mutual") rather than the tighter التبديلية for "commuting" — both are understandable and do not distort the teaching.

examples.suggested: تعيين مناصر/مدافع كمي داخلي للدفاع عن الحوسبة الكمية
examples.why: "بطل" is a literal calque of "champion"; here the role is an advocate, and "المدافع" is already used for the same concept elsewhere in the file. Understandable, not misleading — minor.

examples.suggested: عدد مجموعات مشغِّلات Pauli التبديلية (المتبادِلة في التبديل)
examples.why: "المتبادلة" reads as "mutual/reciprocal"; the precise math sense of commuting operators is التبديلية. Loose but recoverable from context — minor.

editor_note: This reads as genuine native Arabic technical prose, not raw MT: the narrative passages (the one-time-pad walkthrough, the no-cloning explanation, the "Caveats" list) flow idiomatically with natural turns like بعبارة مخففة, في نهاية المطاف, and أحرار في استخدام, and the subtle eavesdropping logic in the tables is rendered faithfully with no semantic drift. Core domain terminology is correct and mostly consistent — كيوبت (qubit), حالة ذاتية (eigenstate), القاعدة/القياس (basis/measurement), نظرية عدم الاستنساخ (no-cloning theorem), عملية وحدوية (unitary), نموذج ضوضاء (noise model), واجهة خلفية (backend), دقة (fidelity), and التنصت/المتنصت (eavesdropping/eavesdropper) is used consistently throughout. The verdict is MINOR_ISSUES because of three within-file inconsistencies a native editor would unify: "one-time pad" appears as three distinct renderings — الوسادة لمرة واحدة (heading + line 88), the dominant لوحة الاستخدام الواحد (~10×), and الرقعة ذات الاستخدام الواحد (Critical-concepts/MC sections, 3×); "basis" is overwhelmingly قاعدة/القواعد (46×) but slips to أساس الاختيار (line 241) and الأسس (line 523) for the same measurement-basis concept; and the "quantum" adjective alternates between الكمومية (body, ~51×) and الكمية (title/frontmatter, Experiments 2–3, Questions, ~25×). A fourth minor point: "quantum teleportation" is الإنتقال الكمومي (line 180) — a nonstandard hamza spelling of الانتقال and a weaker choice than the conventional النقل الآني, reading more as "quantum transition." None of these mislead, invert a claim, or break the pedagogy, and the informal instructional register (جرب, اضغط, لاحظ, أقنع شركاءك) holds throughout.

examples.source: one-time pad

examples.suggested: Pick one rendering (e.g. لوحة الاستخدام الواحد) and use it everywhere, glossing (one-time pad) once
examples.why: The English uses a single fixed term; rendering it three different ways across the same file forces the learner to re-map the central concept and reads as inconsistent editing.

examples.why: The whole protocol uses قاعدة/القواعد for measurement 'basis'; switching to أساس/أسس in the answer block and one body sentence breaks the established term mid-document.

examples.source: quantum (adjective)

examples.suggested: Standardize on one form (الكمومية is the more precise physics adjective) across title and body
examples.why: Both forms are acceptable Arabic, but the file visibly alternates between them, which a native technical editor would unify within one document.

editor_note: The register (informal "ty": musíš, můžeš, najdeš, Chceš-li, Definuj, předej) and the core domain rendering "expectation value → střední hodnota" (L40) are correct and consistent, and no semantic drift exists — meanings track the English faithfully. The file FAILS on terminology, though: the central concept "circuit" is rendered two ways within the same document — correctly as "obvod" in body text (L46, L68) but left as raw English "Circuit" in at least six places (headings L44/L65/L277/L307/L333 and body L48 "Definuj kvantový Circuit v QASM", L136 "snížit chyby Circuit"). Worse, the untranslated nouns break the surrounding Czech inflection, producing phrases no native technical writer would write: "snížit chyby Circuit" (should be "chyby obvodu"), "do tohoto obvod" (missing the -u genitive, L68), "Parametrizované Circuit" (L277), and "přidání parametrizovaných Gate" (L307) — where "Gate" should be the standard Czech "hradlo". These read as un-edited machine output and constitute ≥3 within-file term inconsistencies plus wrong/over-retained domain terminology, which is the FAIL threshold.

examples.source: You need at least one circuit as the input to the Estimator primitive. / Define a QASM quantum circuit.

examples.suggested: Definuj kvantový obvod v QASM
examples.why: Same concept 'circuit' is translated to 'obvod' one line earlier but left as English 'Circuit' here. Inconsistent within-file rendering of a core domain term; the standard Czech term obvod exists and was used.

examples.suggested: ...umožňují uživatelům snížit chyby obvodu modelováním šumu zařízení...
examples.why: 'chyby Circuit' is broken Czech: untranslated English noun with no case agreement; reads as raw MT. Should be 'chyby obvodu' (genitive).

examples.suggested: Vytvoření kvantového obvodu a přidání parametrizovaných hradel
examples.why: Both 'Circuit' and 'Gate' left in English; 'gate' has the conventional Czech term 'hradlo'. Adjective agreement (kvantového Circuit, parametrizovaných Gate) is also broken.

examples.suggested: ...data vysílaná do tohoto obvodu...
examples.why: Grammar error: 'do tohoto obvod' is missing the -u ending required after 'do'. Should be 'do tohoto obvodu'.

editor_note: The prose reads as genuine, fluent Czech technical writing throughout — a native quantum engineer would recognize it. Core terminology is correct and conventional: "unitární matice", "Euklidovská/euklidovská norma", "jednotkový vektor", "Diracova notace", "komplexně sdružená transpozice", "permutační matice", "kvantová Fourierova transformace", and "stav plus"/"stav minus" are all idiomatic and used consistently; "qubit" is correctly kept in English per field convention, and the informal "ty" register holds cleanly (Všimni si, Můžeš, Představ si). No semantic drift: the math claims, the plus/minus discrimination argument, the Born-rule explanation, and the square-root-of-NOT pedagogy all match the English faithfully. The issues are isolated, not misleading: (1) a raw untranslated leak at line 450 where "The operations" was left in English and concatenated as "The operations   Operace"; (2) "absolute value squared" alternates between "absolutních hodnot na druhou" (lines 22/24/42/83/94) and "druhé mocnině absolutní hodnoty"/"absolutní hodnotou na druhou" (lines 257/259/342) — both correct but inconsistent; (3) "qubit" is left undeclined where Czech grammar wants a case ending ("na jednom qubit" → qubitu; "stavů qubit" → qubitu); (4) inconsistent capitalization of "qubit" — lowercase in body but capitalized "Qubitech" in three headings (lines 382, 639) and one body line (384). None of these distort meaning or weaken the teaching, but the English leak and the term/casing inconsistencies are real editorial slips.

examples.source: The operations
examples.translation: The operations   Operace
examples.suggested: Operace
examples.why: Raw untranslated English string left in the file and concatenated with the Czech translation on the same line — a clear MT/integration leak, though it does not change meaning.

examples.source: absolute values squared / absolute value squared
examples.translation: absolutních hodnot na druhou (l.22,24,42,83,94) vs druhé mocnině absolutní hodnoty / absolutní hodnotou na druhou (l.257,259,342)
examples.suggested: Pick one rendering, e.g. consistently "absolutních hodnot na druhou" throughout
examples.why: The same English term is rendered two different (both valid) ways within one file, reducing terminological consistency in teaching material.

examples.translation: na jednom qubit (l.468); na Qubitech (l.382, 384, 639)
examples.suggested: na jednom qubitu; na qubitech (lowercase, declined)
examples.why: "qubit" is left undeclined where Czech grammar requires a case ending, and is capitalized as "Qubitech" in headings while lowercase in the body — a native technical writer would decline it and keep casing consistent.

examples.source: vectors representing quantum states need not have nonnegative real number entries, and it is the sum ... that must equal 1
examples.translation: vektory ... nemusí mít nezáporné reálné složky a musí být roven $1$ součet absolutních hodnot na druhou (nikoli součet samotných složek)

examples.why: The fronted predicate "musí být roven 1 součet ..." is grammatically valid but stiff/calqued from the English cleft; reordering so the subject precedes reads more naturally.

editor_note: The file is clearly stitched from two translation passes that diverge at the "Rozebrání algoritmu QFT" heading (line 419), and the seam produces a within-file terminology split on a core domain term: "computational basis" is rendered "výpočetní báze" 17 times in the first half (the correct, standard Czech term) but switches to the calque "komputační báze" 7 times in the second half (lines 421, 423, 453, 459, 608, 648, 669). "Komputační" is not idiomatic Czech for this concept and no native quantum physicist would write it; the inconsistency alone is disqualifying. "Subroutine" is likewise rendered three ways — "podprogram" (lines 4, 40), "podrutina" (561, 633), and the invented compound "podrutinovým blokem" (559). The second half also carries several grammatical errors a native editor would never leave: line 559 "„korunního klenotech"" should be instrumental singular "korunním klenotem" (it is currently locative plural and ungrammatical), line 421 has both "jaké hradla" (should be neuter "jaká hradla") and "tohoto transformace" (feminine, should be "této transformace"), and line 416 has a duplicated "Kdybys/kdybys". The first half reads fluently and faithfully and the informal "ty" register holds throughout, but the terminology inconsistency on "computational basis" plus the clustered grammar errors put this past MINOR_ISSUES.

examples.source: computational basis
examples.translation: komputační báze (lines 421-669) vs výpočetní báze (lines 76-285)
examples.suggested: výpočetní báze (everywhere)
examples.why: Core domain term rendered two ways within one file; the second-half calque 'komputační báze' is non-standard Czech and breaks consistency with the 17 first-half uses of the correct 'výpočetní báze'.

examples.source: subroutine
examples.translation: podprogram (40) / podrutina (561) / podrutinovým blokem (559)
examples.suggested: podprogram (consistently)
examples.why: 'Subroutine' appears in three different renderings; 'podrutinovým blokem' is an invented compound for plain 'subroutine' and 'podrutina' is a Russianism alongside the cleaner 'podprogram' used earlier.

examples.source: crown jewel
examples.translation: včetně „korunního klenotech" kvantových algoritmů — Shorova algoritmu faktorizace
examples.suggested: včetně „korunního klenotu" kvantových algoritmů — Shorova algoritmu faktorizace
examples.why: 'klenotech' is locative plural and ungrammatical here; the genitive singular 'klenotu' (or instrumental 'klenotem') is required. A native writer would not leave this.

examples.suggested: jaká hradla skutečně implementujeme ... abychom dosáhli této transformace?
examples.why: Two gender-agreement errors in one sentence: 'jaké hradla' (hradlo is neuter → 'jaká hradla') and 'tohoto transformace' (transformace is feminine → 'této transformace').

editor_note: This is a short, mostly procedural setup guide, and the German reads as native technical prose throughout — no translationese. The intro renders "for those who prefer to work in an online development environment" idiomatically as "für alle, die lieber in einer Online-Entwicklungsumgebung arbeiten möchten" rather than a calque, and "submit jobs to IBM QPUs" becomes the conventional "Jobs an IBM QPUs ... übermitteln." Terminology is consistent and correct: "Notebook," "Framework," "Cloud-Dienstleister," "Abhängigkeiten" (dependencies), "Arbeitsbereich" (workspace), and the careful distinction between "vorinstalliert" (preinstalled) and "vorkonfiguriert" (preconfigured) tracking the English. The informal "du" register holds uniformly (deinem/du erstellst/findest du) with no Sie slips, and I found no semantic drift — conditions, quantities, and link labels all match the source. The only trivial flattening is "quantum software development cloud platform" compressing to "Quantum-Software-Entwicklungsplattform" (drops the explicit "cloud"), which is not misleading given qBraid's context. Faithful, well-edited, teaches as clearly as the English.

Solid, mostly native technical prose with the informal "du" register held consistently (weißt du, beginne, passe dich an) and faithful meaning throughout — all quantities (400 Minuten, ~30%, ~10-fach, 120 Qubits, 30–120 Minuten) and negations ("kein schnellerer Allzweckcomputer", "ungelöst") survive intact, and core domain terms are sound and consistent (Superposition, Verschränkung, Interferenz, supraleitende Qubits, Kohärenzzeit, Gate-Treue for "gate fidelity", transpilierte Tiefe, Circuit-Tiefe). The main weakness is the calqued rendering of "utility-scale" as "Nutzungs-Skalen-" coined three ways (Nutzungs-Skalenarbeit L101, Nutzungs-Skalenforschung L110, Nutzungs-Skalenquantencomputing L124) — internally consistent but reads as translationese that no native quantum writer would coin (the field keeps "Utility-Scale" or uses "Nutzen-Skala"). There is also a genuine spelling defect "Hochkvalifikationsindustrien" (L174, should be "Hochqualifikations-") and two small slips: "gemäß dem IBM Quantum Roadmap" (Roadmap is feminine → der) and "positioniert IBM als Führerin" for a company (Marktführer/führend would be natural). None mislead the reader, so the meaning and pedagogy of the English are preserved.

examples.source: unlocking utility-scale work for true innovation

examples.suggested: ermöglichen Arbeit im Utility-Scale-Bereich für echte Innovation
examples.why: "Nutzungs-Skalenarbeit" is a literal calque of "utility-scale work" and reads as translationese; the field keeps "Utility-Scale" or uses a natural paraphrase. Same calque recurs as Nutzungs-Skalenforschung (L110) and Nutzungs-Skalenquantencomputing (L124).

examples.source: catalyzing new high-skill industries

examples.suggested: die Förderung neuer Hochqualifikationsindustrien
examples.why: "Hochkvalifikation" is a misspelling of "Hochqualifikation" — a real spelling defect in the prose.

examples.source: positions IBM as a leader in quantum-centric supercomputing
examples.translation: positioniert IBM als Führerin im quantenzentrierten Supercomputing
examples.suggested: positioniert IBM als führend im quantenzentrierten Supercomputing
examples.why: "Führerin" (feminine personal "leader") for a company is unidiomatic; "führend"/"Marktführer" is what a native writer would use.

editor_note: This reads like native German technical prose, not machine translation: idiomatic touches such as "Stell es dir vor wie den Versuch, eine detaillierte Skulptur nur aus handelsüblichen LEGO-Steinen nachzubauen", "Keine Sorge! Du musst nicht alles selbst programmieren" and the COBYLA analogy ("als könnte er die Landschaft nicht sehen") show real editorial reworking rather than calquing. Core terminology is conventional and faithful — Grundzustandsenergie, Hamiltonoperator, Verschränkung, Superposition, Ansatz/Ansätze, Erwartungswert, Bloch-Kugel, Observable, Quantenschaltkreis — and the informal "du" register holds throughout. No semantic drift: the variational-principle inequality, the "obere Schranke"/"leicht unterhalb" caveat, the 10^48-bit / 10^24-star figures, and the negative reaction-energy conclusion are all preserved. The issues are pure consistency slips, none misleading: (1) "Hamiltonian" is used in the first H-atom prose section (lines 335–343, 368, 582) but "Hamiltonoperator" everywhere else — the file mixes both; (2) the optional appendix lapses into clustered anglicisms ("Circuits", "Quantum Circuits", "Optimizer", "Overhead", lines 1084–1092, 1278) where the body uses Schaltkreise/Optimierer; (3) "Post-process" is rendered as "Nachverarbeitung" in the H-atom Step 4 (line 680) but "Nachbearbeitung" in the H2 Step 4 (line 828); (4) "near-term quantum algorithm" gains an interpolated "(NISQ-Ära)" (line 1019) not present in the English — accurate and learner-friendly, but an addition. Verdict MINOR_ISSUES rather than PASS only because of the section-bound Hamiltonian/Schaltkreis-vs-anglicism inconsistency; meaning and pedagogy are fully intact.

editor_note: This reads as genuine native Spanish technical writing, not machine translation. Idiomatic choices like "la tarea que tienes entre manos" (the task at hand), "el panorama general" (the bigger picture), "lista para usar" (out-of-the-box), and "código estilo primitivo" (primitive-style code) are exactly what a Spanish-speaking quantum engineer would write. Core terminology is correct and perfectly consistent: "primitivos" for primitives throughout, "valores esperados" for expectation values, "muestreo/muestrea" for sampling, "observables", "vector de estado" for statevector, "registro de salida" for output register, "mitigación y supresión de errores", and "posprocesamiento"; field-internal terms (QPUs, backend, Sampler, Estimator) are correctly left in English. Meaning is faithful paragraph by paragraph with no drift, no inverted conditions, and no dropped caveats — the "would not and should not be familiar" nuance survives as "no tenían por qué estar familiarizados", and the pedagogical "one step closer to where classical programming is today" framing is preserved. Informal "tú" register holds cleanly throughout (te permiten, tienes, puedes, Consulta, Revisa) with no usted slips; teaching clarity matches the English.

This is strong, native-sounding technical Spanish that reads as if a quantum engineer wrote it, not as MT: "En analogía con la transformada discreta de Fourier", "En términos simples" for "In Layman's terms", and the natural inversion "Como ejemplo, aplicamos la QFT a |5⟩" all flow idiomatically, and the informal "tú" register (te enseña, recuerdas, considera, observa, implementa, estima) holds throughout. Terminology is conventional and consistent: "puerta(s)" for gate, "registro contador"/"registro objetivo", "vector de estado", "operador unitario", "unitario controlado", and the deliberate retention of "eigenvector"/"eigenvalores" (not autovector/autovalor) matches IBM's Qiskit Spanish convention and is used uniformly. The one real defect is a softened negation at line 148: English "we will NOT use the Qiskit patterns framework UNTIL we move on to quantum phase estimation" became "Demostramos la QFT solo con simuladores Y UTILIZAMOS el framework Qiskit Patterns cuando pasemos a la estimación de fase cuántica" — the "not...until" caveat is dropped and recast as a plain affirmative; the meaning is still recoverable from the "cuando pasemos a QPE" clause, so this is a minor flattening rather than an inversion. No terminology errors and no other drift.

examples.source: We will demonstrate QFT using simulators only, and thus we will not use the Qiskit patterns framework until we move on to quantum phase estimation.
examples.translation: Demostramos la QFT solo con simuladores y utilizamos el framework Qiskit Patterns cuando pasemos a la estimación de fase cuántica.
examples.suggested: Demostramos la QFT solo con simuladores y, por tanto, no usaremos el framework Qiskit Patterns hasta que pasemos a la estimación de fase cuántica.
examples.why: The English 'not...until' negation is dropped and recast as a plain affirmative ('y utilizamos'), weakening the explicit caveat that the QFT section deliberately avoids the Patterns framework. Meaning is recoverable from the temporal clause but the emphasis is flattened.

examples.source: We try to apply QFT to |5⟩ as an example.
examples.translation: Como ejemplo, aplicamos la QFT a |5⟩.
examples.why: Positive example of native fluency: the translator fronted 'Como ejemplo' and dropped the awkward literal 'We try to apply', producing exactly what a Spanish technical writer would write. No change needed.

editor_note: The connected prose is genuinely fluent, idiomatic, and faithful: the intuition about quantum parallelism, phase-kickback, balanced/constant functions, and the deterministic-vs-probabilistic speedup nuance all survive intact, the informal "tú" register holds throughout (consulta, piensa, preferirías, recuerda), and conventional choices like leaving "shots" and "little endian" in English are correct. The FAIL is driven entirely by a core-terminology inconsistency: "gate" is rendered three different ways — the correct Spanish "puerta" ("la puerta $U_f$", lines 73 and 587) but also the untranslated, capitalized "Gate" mid-sentence ("la siguiente Gate" L69, "la Gate Hadamard" L176, "una Gate Hadamard ... una Gate NOT" L575). Parallel to this, "circuit" appears once as the untranslated capitalized "Circuit" ("un diagrama de Circuit", L256) while every other occurrence is correctly "circuito" (L60/69/77/176/190...). These look like glossary-protection leakage, not a defensible English-retention decision — the file itself proves it knows the right word ("puerta", "circuito") — and in otherwise-clean prose the bare "Gate"/"Circuit" tokens read as raw translation artifacts a native quantum writer would never leave. A secondary, non-blocking awkwardness: "Las primeras y últimas funciones" (L247) for "The first and the last functions" would read better as the singular "La primera y la última función".

editor_note: This reads as genuine native French technical prose, not machine translation: sentences flow naturally (e.g. "ce qui facilite la prise de décisions lors des transformations" for "making it easier to make transformation decisions") and the translator even improves the EN by merging the two-sentence "This is how the transpiler pipeline operates. A circuit is taken..." into a single colon-linked sentence ("C'est ainsi que fonctionne le pipeline du transpileur : un circuit est pris en entrée, traité sous forme de DAG, et un circuit transformé est produit en sortie"). Domain terminology is correct and fully consistent within the file: "transpileur", "passe(s) de transpileur", "carte de couplage" (coupling map), "chemin de swap", "portes SWAP", "qubit", "nœud(s)", "arêtes orientées", "sommets", and "DAG/DAGs" with "netlist" rightly kept in English. The informal "tu" register holds throughout the imperative instructions and the Recommendations admonition (Utilise, Installe, Consulte, Apprends, Essaie) with no vous/veuillez slips. The only thing approaching a quibble is "pour rapprocher les qubits concernés" for "to bring the involved qubits into adjacency", which slightly relaxes the precise notion of adjacency to "bring closer", but it remains accurate and pedagogically clear in context; no semantic drift, dropped caveats, or inverted claims were found.

Strong, faithful technical translation that reads largely as native French mathematical prose. Domain terminology is correct and consistent throughout: "produit scalaire" (inner product), "conjugué transposé" (conjugate transpose), "norme euclidienne", "base orthonormale"/"ensemble orthonormal" (with correct gender agreement maintained across both forms), "matrices hermitiennes", "idempotentes", "mesures dans la base standard", "mesures projectives", "matrice par blocs", "procédé d'orthogonalisation de Gram–Schmidt", "vecteur unitaire" for unit vector, and "qubit" kept in English — all conventional choices, used consistently. Register is correctly informal ("Rappelle-toi", "Note que") with the usual impersonal "on" of French math writing; no vous/veuillez slips. No semantic drift: conditions, quantities, and the Bell-basis/projection examples all match the English. The one real defect is a repeated ungrammatical construction "Pour quel que soit le résultat $k$ que la mesure produit" (lines 494 and 521), a stiff calque of "For whichever outcome k the measurement produces"; a native writer would drop "Pour" and write "Quel que soit le résultat $k$ produit par la mesure". A milder anglicism, "On a également que le produit..." (line 51, calquing "We also have that"), is acceptable in math register but slightly stilted. Neither issue misleads the reader, so this is MINOR.

examples.source: For whichever outcome $k$ the measurement produces, the state of $\mathsf{X}$ becomes

examples.suggested: Quel que soit le résultat $k$ produit par la mesure, l'état de $\mathsf{X}$ devient
examples.why: "Pour quel que soit" is ungrammatical in French — a calque of English "For whichever". The construction repeats verbatim at the parallel projective-measurement passage (line 521). A native technical writer drops "Pour" and uses "Quel que soit ... produit par la mesure". Meaning is recoverable, so naturalness-only.

examples.source: We also have that the product of a bra vector and a ket vector ... results in a scalar.

examples.why: "On a également que" is a tolerable-but-stilted calque of "We also have that"; acceptable in math register but a touch anglicized. Minor — included for completeness, not load-bearing.

editor_note: This reads as genuinely native technical French written by someone who understands the physics, not machine output: idiomatic choices like "plonger dans", "regardera sous le capot", "joyau de la couronne", "À ton avis" and "rappelons-nous" land naturally, and the informal "tu/ton/on" register is held consistently throughout with no vous/veuillez slips. Core domain terminology is correct and consistent: qubit (kept), porte (gate), base computationnelle, valeur propre / état propre / vecteur propre (eigenvalue/eigenstate/eigenvector), Hamiltonien, backend (kept), and "rétroaction de phase" glossed once with the English "phase kickback" then used consistently. The pedagogical intuition (the Russian-nesting-doll / "poupée russe" analogy, the width-inverse-proportionality explanation, the phase-winding narrative) is preserved with the same clarity and emphasis as the English; no semantic drift, softened claims, or broken examples were found. The only real blemishes are minor and never mislead: "momentum" is rendered as "impulsion" in the position/momentum pairing (line 44, 282) but as "quantité de mouvement" in the Heisenberg sentence (line 282) — a small within-file inconsistency between two correct French physics terms — and there are a couple of stray mid-sentence capitalizations ("exécuter notre Circuit" line 124, "avec un seul Qubit" line 427). (Note: the QFT/CP/SWAP matrix blocks at lines 483-513 carry escaped LaTeX braces like \text\{QFT\}_4, but that is math-markup escaping, out of scope for the prose judgment.)

editor_note: This is a cloud/networking guide (VPE for VPC), so the quantum-term checklist is largely N/A; the load-bearing terms are infrastructure terms (VPE, VPC, endpoint gateway, subnet, CRN, FQDN, backbone), and the translation correctly and consistently keeps them in Latin script — the conventional choice for Hebrew technical writing. The prose reads as genuinely native: "ממשקי IP וירטואליים הקשורים ל-endpoint gateway", "שמתרחבת אופקית, יתירה וזמינה ביותר" (scales horizontally, redundant, highly available), and "בגב הרשת הפרטית" (on the private backbone) are all idiomatic engineer-Hebrew, not calqued MT. Meaning is faithful throughout, including the faithful mirroring of IBM's own quirky source line "ניתן לגשת ל-IBM Quantum Platform דרך הרשת הציבורית בלבד". Register is consistently informal (שלך, imperatives בצע/ציין/השתמש). The only blemishes are cosmetic: L33 "והCRNים" attaches the ה- prefix and Hebrew plural directly to a Latin token (cleaner would be "וה-CRN-ים"), and the section mixes impersonal forms ("רוצים להתחבר", "משתמשים") with imperatives elsewhere — both informal, non-misleading. PASS.

examples.why: Attaching the Hebrew definite article and plural ending directly onto the Latin token 'CRN' (והCRNים) reads awkwardly; standard Hebrew technical typography inserts a maqaf to separate the Hebrew affixes from Latin script. Purely cosmetic, does not affect meaning.

examples.source: ...the region in which you want connect... / follow these steps:

examples.why: The file mixes impersonal plural ('רוצים', 'משתמשים', L33) with second-person imperative ('בצע', 'ציין', 'השתמש', L42/61/72). Both are informal and acceptable, but a single consistent address form would read more polished. Minor, non-misleading.

editor_note: The translation reads as if two translators split the file and never reconciled their glossaries, producing systematic within-file inconsistency on the three most central domain terms. "kernel" — the subject of the entire lesson — is rendered ליבה (and מטריצת הליבה) throughout the first half (title שיטות ליבה קוונטיות, intro, Steps 1-4, "Single kernel matrix entry"), then silently flips to גרעין (מטריצת הגרעין) for the whole "Full kernel matrix," "Scaling," and "Review" sections (e.g. the Review heading reads חישוב גרעין קוונטי כולל). "feature map" similarly drifts from מיפוי מאפיינים → מפת מאפיינים → מפת תכונות / מפות תכונות, so even "feature" itself flips from מאפיינים to תכונות mid-document. "circuit" is kept as English Circuit/Circuits for most of the file but becomes Hebrew מעגל/מעגלים from the zz_feature_map section onward — so the same concept appears two ways. These are three independent core-term inconsistencies, well past the FAIL threshold of ≥3, and a reader moving from one section to the next would not realize ליבה and גרעין denote the same object. On top of the consistency failure there is a genuine mistranslation at the Step 3 simulator note: English "The syntax for running on a simulator is commented out below" became התחביר להרצה על סימולטור מוזנח להלן, where מוזנח means "neglected/abandoned," not "commented out" (it should be מסומן כהערה). Register is otherwise correctly informal (נתחיל, שימו לב, נסו) and most prose is fluent and faithful, but the terminology chaos on kernel/feature/circuit is disqualifying for teaching material whose whole point is one consistent concept.

examples.source: # Quantum Kernels / ## Full kernel matrix ... Calculating a quantum kernel involves
examples.translation: # ליבות קוונטיות ... ## מטריצת ליבה מלאה ... חישוב גרעין קוונטי כולל
examples.suggested: Pick ONE rendering of 'kernel' and use it throughout — e.g. ליבה everywhere (title already uses שיטות ליבה קוונטיות), so the Review must read חישוב ליבה קוונטית כולל, not חישוב גרעין קוונטי
examples.why: 'kernel' is the lesson's central term but is ליבה in the first half and גרעין in the second; a learner cannot tell they are the same object. ≥3 such splits = FAIL.

examples.source: feature map / Try different feature maps and entanglement schemes
examples.translation: מיפוי מאפיינים (early) → מפת מאפיינים → מפת תכונות / נסו מפות תכונות
examples.suggested: Standardize on one of מיפוי מאפיינים or מפת מאפיינים for 'feature map' and one of מאפיינים/תכונות for 'feature' across the whole file
examples.why: Both the head noun (מיפוי vs מפה) and the word for 'feature' (מאפיינים vs תכונות) flip between halves, compounding the inconsistency.

examples.source: the depths of pre-coded encoding circuits ... monitor circuit depth
examples.translation: העומק של Circuits ... (later) עומק המעגל / עומקי המעגלים
examples.suggested: Either keep English Circuit consistently or use Hebrew מעגל consistently — not both

examples.source: The syntax for running on a simulator is commented out below.
examples.translation: התחביר להרצה על סימולטור מוזנח להלן.
examples.suggested: התחביר להרצה על סימולטור מסומן כהערה (מושבת כהערה) להלן.
examples.why: מוזנח means 'neglected/abandoned'; it does not convey 'commented out' and misleads the reader about why the code below is inert.

examples.source: This could be an indicator that these two data points are somehow similar in nature
examples.translation: זה עשוי להעיד על כך ששתי נקודות הנתונים דומות כלשהו בטבען
examples.suggested: ... ששתי נקודות הנתונים דומות במידת מה באופיין
examples.why: דומות כלשהו is an unnatural placement of כלשהו; a native technical writer would write במידת מה / באופן כלשהו and prefer באופיין over בטבען.

editor_note: Overall the prose is fluent, informal, and semantically faithful: domain terminology is mostly correct and consistent — "ערך עצמי"/"וקטור עצמי"/"מצב עצמי" (eigenvalue/eigenvector/eigenstate), "האמילטוניאן" (Hamiltonian), "סופרפוזיציה", "פאזה", "אומדן/אמידת פאזה" (phase estimation), and the musical "דו אמצעי" (middle C) are all handled well, and the matryoshka analogy and phase-kickback explanation teach as clearly as the English. The main naturalness defect is that the translator coins a non-existent Hebrew verb from "Fourier transform": "לטרנספורם פורייה את צורת הגל" (line 49), "לטרנספורם קבוצה דיסקרטית" (69), "בואו נטרנספורם פורייה את המצב" (233, 345) — a native technical writer would write "לבצע טרנספורם פורייה על..."; this calque recurs 6+ times in load-bearing teaching sentences and reads as MT. Second, "circuit" is rendered inconsistently as kept-English "Circuit" (lines 121, 512, 521, 600) AND as Hebrew "מעגל" ("ספריית המעגלים", line 119) in the same file. Minor: "qubit"/"Qubit" casing of the kept-English term flips arbitrarily within single sentences (e.g. line 82, 512). None of these mislead the reader, so this is not FAIL, but they should be cleaned up. (Note: the escaped braces in the $\text\{QFT\}_4$ matrices on lines 479-510 are a math-rendering corruption outside prose scope and not counted here.)

examples.source: We can Fourier transform this waveform to go from the time basis to the frequency basis:
examples.translation: אנחנו יכולים לטרנספורם פורייה את צורת הגל הזאת כדי לעבור מבסיס הזמן לבסיס התדירות:
examples.suggested: אנחנו יכולים לבצע טרנספורם פורייה על צורת הגל הזאת כדי לעבור מבסיס הזמן לבסיס התדירות:
examples.why: Hebrew has no verb 'לטרנספורם'; this is an invented conjugation of the English noun used as a verb. The grammatical Hebrew is 'לבצע טרנספורם פורייה על...'. The coinage recurs (lines 69, 177, 233, 345) and reads as machine translation.

examples.why: The same core term 'circuit' is rendered as kept-English 'Circuit' in most of the file but as Hebrew 'מעגל' in 'ספריית המעגלים' — an internal inconsistency for a primary domain term.

examples.suggested: Standardize the casing of the kept-English term ('qubit'/'qubits') everywhere.

editor_note: This reads like native Indonesian developer documentation, not raw MT: the intro sentence "memungkinkan kamu menentukan timing relatif operasi alih-alih timing absolut" and "Compiler berusaha meminimalkan durasi stretch, dengan memperhatikan constraint timing pada satu atau lebih qubit" track the English meaning precisely while flowing naturally. Domain terminology is correct and consistent throughout: "stretch" (kept as the OpenQASM type name), "qubit/gate/circuit/barrier/delay/compiler/constraint/timing" retained in English per the prevailing ID quantum-doc convention, and the translated terms are stable across the file — "remainder" is always "sisa", "goal value" always "nilai tujuan", and "resolution/resolved" consistently as "resolusi/diselesaikan". No semantic drift: the emphasized negation in the admonition ("ini BUKAN waktu yang digunakan untuk penagihan"), the left-alignment claim ("disejajarkan ke kiri, apapun durasi aktualnya"), and every numeric example (8+2=10, resolve-to-25-with-remainder-1, the 1365+10=1375 walkthrough) are faithfully preserved. The informal "kamu" register holds consistently with no deferential bapak/ibu slips. The only cosmetic nit is occasional anglicized gerund-as-modifier phrasing like "left-aligning urutan gate", which is defensible in technical ID prose and not a defect.

This is faithful, fluent technical Indonesian that teaches as clearly as the English, with consistent terminology and the informal inclusive-"kita" register held throughout (no deferential bapak/ibu forms). Core domain terms are handled well and consistently: "qubit", "query", "string", "phase kickback", and "input/output" are correctly left in English (standard ID practice), while "produk titik biner" (binary dot product), "produk tensor" (tensor product), "keadaan basis standar" (standard basis states), and "paritas" (parity) are the right conventional renderings, all used consistently. The translator made an unusual but fully consistent glossary choice to keep "Circuit" and "Gate" capitalized in English everywhere (e.g. "Circuit kuantum", "Gate Hadamard", "Circuit Deutsch-Jozsa") rather than using "sirkuit"/"gerbang"; this is defensible and never inconsistent, so it is at most a minor naturalness note, not a defect. The only soft spots: "modest" advantage is rendered as "sederhana" (literally "simple") at lines 13 and 177 where "kecil/sedikit" would convey magnitude better — though it is used consistently — and "ngomong-ngomong" for "by the way" (line 103) is markedly colloquial, matching the casual English but reading slightly conversational for a textbook. I checked the EN typo at line 192 ("50% change") and the translation correctly reads it as "peluang 50%" (chance), so no drift was introduced. No semantic drift, inverted conditions, or term inconsistencies found.

examples.source: Here's a quantum circuit description... / a single layer of Hadamard gates / the Deutsch-Jozsa circuit
examples.translation: Berikut deskripsi Circuit kuantum... / satu lapisan Gate Hadamard / Circuit Deutsch-Jozsa
examples.suggested: sirkuit kuantum / lapisan gerbang Hadamard / sirkuit Deutsch-Jozsa
examples.why: "Circuit" and "Gate" are left capitalized in English throughout rather than using the conventional ID terms "sirkuit"/"gerbang". The choice is applied 100% consistently, so it does not mislead — flagged only as a naturalness/convention note; a native technical writer would more often write "sirkuit" and "gerbang".

examples.source: the advantage is quite modest / a rather modest advantage
examples.translation: keuntungannya cukup sederhana / keuntungan yang cukup sederhana
examples.why: "sederhana" means "simple", not "modest/small in magnitude". The English is about the SIZE of the advantage (one query vs two), so "keuntungan yang cukup kecil" or "agak sedikit" would carry the quantitative nuance better. Consistent across both occurrences, so meaning survives, but the emphasis on smallness is slightly flattened.

examples.source: Here, by the way, we're writing binary strings of length n as...
examples.translation: Di sini, ngomong-ngomong, kita menulis string biner panjang n sebagai...
examples.suggested: Di sini, sebagai catatan, kita menulis string biner panjang n sebagai...
examples.why: "ngomong-ngomong" is a markedly spoken/colloquial connective. It does match the casual English "by the way", but for written course material a slightly more neutral aside ("sebagai catatan", "omong-omong") reads more like a technical writer. Minor register slip, not misleading.

editor_note: This reads like genuine Indonesian technical writing, not machine output: the informal register ("kamu", "kita", and first-person "aku/denganmu" in the one-time-pad narration) holds consistently with zero bapak/ibu deferential slips, and idiomatic touches like "untuk dikatakan dengan sopan" (mildly), "wajar untuk bertanya", and "jauh sebelum berbagi rahasia terenkripsi" are well chosen. Core terminology is confident and conventional — "qubit", "basis", "eigenstate", "noise", "fidelitas", "one-time pad", and "teorema no-cloning" are all kept/rendered correctly and used consistently. The only real defects are mild inconsistencies, not mistranslations: the word for quantum "state" drifts from "keadaan" in the early sections (e.g. "keadaan Alice", "keadaan kuantum") to the English "state" in Experiments 2-3 and the Critical-concepts list (e.g. "merekonstruksi state", "state awal", "ensembel state kuantum"); "channel" is "saluran kuantum" throughout but flips to "kanal kuantum" once at the end of Experiment 2 (line 700); "over many kilometers" is slightly strengthened to "ribuan kilometer" (thousands) at line 184; and line 956 has a stray capitalization "qubit-Qubit". None of these mislead the reader about the QKD protocol or the physics, so this is MINOR_ISSUES rather than FAIL.

examples.why: The same domain term "state" is rendered "keadaan" in QKD steps 1-3 and the no-eavesdropper experiment, then switches to English "state" in Experiment 2-3 and the Critical-concepts list (lines 610, 945-957). Within-file inconsistency, though never ambiguous in context.

examples.suggested: saluran kuantum
examples.why: "channel" is consistently "saluran" everywhere else (e.g. "saluran kuantum", "saluran klasik" at lines 244-247) but becomes "kanal kuantum" once at line 700, an isolated term inconsistency.

editor_note: This reads as native, well-edited Italian technical prose, not machine translation. Idioms are genuinely idiomatic ("Dietro le quinte", "vale la pena notare", "Fai pulizia quando hai finito") and the calque-prone "without requiring quantum expertise" becomes the natural "senza necessitare di competenze specifiche in ambito quantistico". Terminology follows the standard Italian QC convention of keeping core English terms (qubit, ensemble, learner, feature, dataset, backend, boosting/bagging/stacking) with Italian articles, and key renderings are internally consistent: "quantum-enhanced" is "potenziato quantisticamente" in all six occurrences, "accuracy" is "accuratezza", and algorithm names (VQE, QAOA) are kept English as the field does. The informal "tu" register holds throughout (puoi, Autenticati, suddividilo, utilizzerai, Consulta, Richiedi, Assicurati) with no Lei/utilizzi slips. No semantic drift: "Il numero di qubit determina soltanto la dimensione dell'ensemble" correctly places the restrictive "soltanto", and the closing caveat ("questa funzionalità non è ancora stata implementata") is preserved. The single nitpick is "Grid stability" rendered as "Stabilità della rete" in the benchmark table, where the source means the electrical power grid; "rete" alone is slightly ambiguous (vs. "rete elettrica") but not misleading in context. Faithful and teaches as clearly as the English.

editor_note: This reads as genuine native technical Italian, not machine translation: idiomatic choices like "i mattoni fondamentali", "una sottigliezza", "evidenzieremo alcune delle caratteristiche principali", and "la giusta combinazione di 0 e 1" are exactly what an Italian-speaking quantum educator would write, and the informal "tu" register (avrai, conosci, ricorda, torna indietro) is held consistently with zero Lei/formal slips. There is a deliberate, clean register split — the classical and qubit sections use Italian "porte" for gates and "calcolo quantistico", while from the "gate → gate quantistici" section onward the file switches to English "gate" and "quantum computing"; both are accepted Italian conventions and each is used consistently within its zone, so this is defensible rather than an inconsistency, though the mid-document switch is a minor stylistic seam. The only real prose blemishes are two calqued constructions that a native writer would smooth: "il rumore causerà ad alcune esecuzioni di produrre errori" (line 744, awkward "causerà a... di") and "l'altro è garantito di essere trovato" (line 247, calque of "is guaranteed to be found"); neither distorts meaning. editor_note: The prose is fluent, native-sounding technical Japanese — paragraphs like the Heron description (「Eagle サイズへとアップグレードされた Egret であり、Osprey で以前に展開された信号配信における重要なイノベーションを取り込んでいます」) read as something a Japanese quantum engineer wrote, not raw MT. Two non-misleading slips keep this from a clean PASS. Second, register slips into keigo against the informal desu/masu convention: 「ご使用ください」and「ご確認いただけます」in the same Admonition (本文 should use simple 使用してください/確認できます). editor_note: This is fluent, genuinely native-sounding technical Japanese throughout: connectives (したがって/なお/つまり/一方), topic marking, and the consistent polite desu/masu register with no keigo all read as written by a Japanese crypto/quantum engineer, not as machine output. Fluency is consistently strong: passages such as "증거가 점점 쌓여 가고 있었습니다", "모든 고전적 직관을 뒤흔드는 이상한 일일 것입니다", and the pedagogical aside "이것이 사소해 보일 수도 있습니다. 결국 ... 생각할 수 있습니다" are idiomatic and well-cadenced, with no calqued English syntax. editor_note: Solid, faithful translation that reads as competent technical Malay with no semantic drift: "kelebihan kuantum" (quantum advantage), "litar kuantum", "subruang", "tenaga keadaan asas", "qubit logik" and "seni bina heterogen" are all correct, conventional, and used consistently, and Sampler/Estimator/gate are sensibly kept in English. editor_note: The single most important domain term in this document — "entanglement" / "entangle(d)" — is rendered in at least three mutually incompatible ways within one file, which is a serious failure for a teleportation tutorial that hinges on this concept. The introduction and theory sections use the "pemerangkapan / merangkap / terperangkap" family (e.g. L46, L86-87, L230, L258), but L60 and L240 also use the odd verb "diperam" (literally "incubated/fermented" — not a recognized Malay rendering of entangle), and then the entire "Konsep penting" / Questions block (L635-638, L667, L674, L688) abruptly switches to a completely different root, "belitan / dibelit / belit". A student reading "diperam," "merangkap," and "dibelit" cannot tell these refer to the same physics. On top of the term split, the prose is littered with machine-stamping artifacts that no native technical writer would produce: random mid-word capitalization in "qubit-Qubit" (L79, L86, L303, L605) and "Qubitnya" (L85, L258, L292, L299), inconsistent "Gate"/"gate", and untranslated capitalized "Circuit" and "Backend" throughout. Sentence-level meaning is mostly faithful and individual sentences read acceptably, but the terminological chaos plus pervasive casing noise pushes this below the consistency bar. The verdict is FAIL on wrong/over-translated domain terminology ("diperam") combined with four-plus within-file inconsistencies on the core term.

examples.source: two particles become linked ... quantum entanglement / bit kuantum boleh "entangled"
examples.translation: prinsip pemerangkapan kuantum ... bit kuantum boleh "diperam"
examples.suggested: prinsip belitan kuantum ... bit kuantum boleh "dibelit"
examples.why: "entanglement" is rendered as pemerangkapan in the intro but as belitan in Critical Concepts, and L60/L240 use "diperam" (incubated/fermented) which is not a correct Malay term for entangle. The core concept must use one consistent, correct term across the whole file.

examples.source: Qubits can be entangled ... Entanglement differs from classical correlations
examples.translation: Qubit boleh dibelit ... Belitan berbeza daripada korelasi klasik
examples.suggested: Use the SAME root chosen earlier in the file (e.g. belitan) everywhere, instead of switching from pemerangkapan/merangkap/diperam to belitan only in this section
examples.why: Within-file term split: the Questions/Critical-concepts block uses belit/belitan while everything before it uses pemerangkapan/merangkap/diperam, so the reader sees three-plus unrelated words for one idea.

examples.source: if the qubits were in the state |11>

examples.suggested: jika qubit-qubit berada dalam keadaan

examples.translation: dua jenis gate kuantum: Gate Hadamard, dan gate CNOT

editor_note: The prose reads as genuinely native technical Polish: it flows naturally, holds the informal "ty" register consistently (postępuj, dołącz, zainicjuj, przejrzyj), and the core terminology is mostly correct and consistent — "qubit", "obwód" (circuit), "bramki" (gates), "pomiary" (measurements), "backend", "shotów" (declined loanword), and "wartości oczekiwane" for expectation values are all the conventional renderings, and "twirling" is sensibly kept in English with Polish declension. The defect that fails this file is a domain-term mistranslation: English "single-qubit" is rendered "jednobitowy" (single-BIT) at L299 ("z dokładnością do jednobitowych bramek") and again at L422 ("jednobitowych operatorów Pauliego-Z"). A bit is not a qubit, so this is both a semantic error and an internal inconsistency, since the same file elsewhere correctly writes "trzy-qubitowy", "10-qubitowy", and "dwu-qubitowe" — it should read "jednokubitowy"/"jednoqubitowy". Secondary, non-failing nits: "mitygacja błędów" for "error mitigation" is a borderline calque (native would lean to "łagodzenie/redukcja błędów") though used consistently, and "Post-przetwarzanie" (L197/L422) for "post-process" is a stiff calque a native writer would phrase differently.

examples.source: Their content is equal, up to single-qubit gates.
examples.translation: Ich zawartość jest równa, z dokładnością do jednobitowych bramek.
examples.suggested: Ich zawartość jest równa, z dokładnością do bramek jednokubitowych.
examples.why: "single-qubit" mistranslated as "jednobitowy" (single-BIT). A bit is not a qubit; this is a domain-term error and contradicts the file's own correct "qubitowy" usage elsewhere.

examples.source: estimate the expectation values of single-qubit Pauli-Z operators
examples.translation: estymować wartości oczekiwane jednobitowych operatorów Pauliego-Z
examples.suggested: estymować wartości oczekiwane jednokubitowych operatorów Pauliego-Z
examples.why: Same single-qubit->jednobitowy (single-bit) error recurs, making the wrong rendering internally inconsistent with "trzy-qubitowy"/"10-qubitowy" used in the same document.

examples.source: Analyze: Post-process and analyze the results.
examples.translation: Analiza: Post-przetwarzanie i analizowanie wyników. / Na koniec post-przetwarzaj wyniki
examples.suggested: Analiza: przetwarzanie końcowe i analiza wyników. / Na koniec przetwórz końcowo wyniki
examples.why: "Post-przetwarzanie"/"post-przetwarzaj" is a hyphenated calque of "post-process" that a native technical writer would render as "przetwarzanie końcowe"; not misleading, but translationese.

editor_note: The prose itself is largely native, fluent technical Polish — the lattice analogies (klocki konstrukcyjne, diament, Wieża Eiffla) teach as clearly as the English, and the informal "ty" register (Wyobraź sobie, otrzymasz, możesz budować) is held consistently with zero Pan/Pani slips. The FAIL is driven by terminology inconsistency on the document's central concept plus a dropped paragraph, not by bad writing. "Quantum-safe cryptography" is rendered three different ways within one file: "kryptografia odporna na komputery kwantowe" (title/intro/headings), "kryptografia kwantowo bezpieczna" (unhyphenated, lines 35/47/57/60), and "kryptografia kwantowo-bezpieczna" (hyphenated, lines 427/435/440/509/513/517). The NP complexity classes are also split — "NP-hard" is kept in English at lines 53-60 but translated as "NP-trudny" at 145/152/206/239, and "NP-intermediate" appears both untranslated (53/55) and as "NP-pośrednich" (517); likewise "module lattices" is "kraty modułowe" (422) vs "kratach modularnych" (519), and the worked-example character is "Alicja" in the LWE-math section (186-247) but "Alice" in the Python illustration prose (293-472). Separately, the English summary's closing sentence ("As we navigate the complex landscape... pivotal in upholding the integrity and confidentiality of our digital interactions in the quantum era") is entirely absent from the Polish — a dropped source paragraph. Finally, line 31 flattens the English's three-synonym list (quantum-safe / quantum-resistant / post-quantum) by translating both "quantum-safe" and "quantum-resistant" as "odporna na komputery kwantowe," so the Polish reads as a tautology ("also called cryptography-resistant-to-quantum-computers"). None of these mislead on the technical content, but they cluster well past the ≥3 within-file inconsistency threshold and include a genuine omission.

examples.source: quantum-safe cryptography
examples.translation: kryptografia odporna na komputery kwantowe (title) / kryptografia kwantowo bezpieczna (l.35) / kryptografia kwantowo-bezpieczna (l.427)
examples.suggested: Pick one convention for the whole file — e.g. 'kryptografia kwantowo-bezpieczna' (hyphenated) used consistently, or 'odporna na komputery kwantowe' consistently.
examples.why: The document's titular concept is rendered three ways within one file. Each is individually correct, but the mix is exactly the within-file inconsistency the criteria flag, and a native technical editor would normalize it.

examples.source: NP-hard / NP-intermediate
examples.translation: kept as 'NP-hard'/'NP-intermediate' (l.53-60) but elsewhere 'NP-trudny' (l.145/206) and 'NP-pośrednich' (l.517)
examples.suggested: Decide once: either keep the English class names everywhere or translate them everywhere (NP-trudny, NP-pośredni, NP-zupełny), then apply uniformly.

examples.source: As we navigate the complex landscape of quantum-safe cryptography, embracing these innovative solutions will be pivotal in upholding the integrity and confidentiality of our digital interactions in the quantum era.
examples.translation: (missing — the Polish summary ends at the 'kraty modularne' paragraph)
examples.suggested: Add the closing sentence, e.g. 'Poruszając się po złożonym krajobrazie kryptografii kwantowo-bezpiecznej, przyjęcie tych innowacyjnych rozwiązań będzie kluczowe dla zachowania integralności i poufności naszych cyfrowych interakcji w erze kwantowej.'

examples.source: This is also sometimes called quantum-resistant, or post-quantum cryptography.
examples.translation: Jest ona również czasami nazywana kryptografią odporną na komputery kwantowe lub kryptografią postkwantową.
examples.suggested: Render 'quantum-resistant' distinctly, e.g. 'kryptografią kwantowoodporną' or by keeping the English term, so the three-synonym list does not collapse into a tautology.

examples.source: This is in contrast to the symmetric key and cryptographic hashing protocols...
examples.translation: Jest to w przeciwieństwie do protokołów klucza symetrycznego oraz kryptograficznego haszowania...
examples.suggested: W przeciwieństwie do tego, protokoły klucza symetrycznego oraz haszowania kryptograficznego, na które wpływają algorytmy Grovera i BHT, ...
examples.why: 'Jest to w przeciwieństwie do' is a literal calque of the English 'This is in contrast to'; a native writer fronts the contrast with 'W przeciwieństwie do tego' or restructures the sentence.

examples.source: Alice

editor_note: The file splits into two registers. The first half (through the "Komputery kwantowe" section, up to ~line 300) is genuinely native, well-edited Polish: gates are "bramka NOT/AND/OR/XOR", circuit is "obwód półsumatora", "spooky action" is the conventional "upiorne działanie na odległość", and the informal "ty" holds throughout. But from the "Gates → quantum gates" heading onward (~line 305 to the end) the translation collapses into raw machine-translation style, leaving core domain terms in English with Polish suffixes glued on and capitalized as proper nouns — and crucially CONTRADICTING the correct terms used earlier. "Gate" is rendered as bramka in the first half but as untranslated "Gate'y/Gate'ów/Gate'em/Gate Hadamarda/Gate Toffoliego" in the second; "circuit" goes from "obwód" to "Circuit'u/Circuits"; "qubit" goes from uninflected lowercase "qubit" to capitalized declined "Qubity/Qubitów"; "transpiler"→"Transpiler", "backend"→"Backend". The summary even annotates the Polish with the English, backwards ("Bramki kwantowe (Gate)", "obwód kwantowy (Circuit)"). The mixing also breaks Polish grammar: "Ten bramka zamienia" and "bramka działający" (feminine noun forced into masculine agreement by the English "Gate"), the nonsense hybrid "rozłożyć na bramka'y CNOT", the typo "obwóds kwantowe", and the non-word adverb "gwarantowanie" calqued from "is guaranteed". No factual/semantic drift was found — truth tables, little-endian reasoning, and the half-adder mapping are all faithful — so this fails on terminology and raw-MT prose, not on meaning. With five domain terms each rendered two incompatible ways within one file, this is well past the FAIL threshold and needs a terminology pass over the entire second half to bring it in line with the clean first half ("bramka", "obwód", lowercase declined "kubit").

examples.source: quantum gates play the same role
examples.translation: kwantowe Gate'y odgrywają tę samą rolę
examples.suggested: kwantowe bramki odgrywają tę samą rolę
examples.why: "gate" is correctly rendered "bramka" in the first half (bramka NOT/AND/XOR) but switches to untranslated, capitalized "Gate'y" here. No Polish physicist writes "Gate Hadamarda"; the standard is "bramka Hadamarda". Inconsistent within the same file.

examples.source: creating a circuit with an X gate looks like this
examples.translation: tworzenie Circuit'u z Gate'em $X$ wygląda następująco
examples.suggested: tworzenie obwodu z bramką $X$ wygląda następująco
examples.why: "circuit" was "obwód" throughout the first half (obwód półsumatora) but is left as English "Circuit'u" here. The summary even writes "obwód kwantowy (Circuit)", annotating the Polish with English — backwards.

examples.source: This gate swaps the states of two qubits.
examples.translation: Ten bramka zamienia stany dwóch Qubitów.
examples.suggested: Ta bramka zamienia stany dwóch kubitów.
examples.why: Grammar breaks because the English "Gate" (masculine) clashes with feminine "bramka": "Ten bramka" should be "Ta bramka". Same failure at "bramka działający" (line 501) and the hybrid non-word "bramka'y" (line 534).

examples.translation: dwa wejściowe Qubity zawsze odwzorowują się na dwa wyjściowe Qubity
examples.suggested: dwa wejściowe kubity zawsze odwzorowują się na dwa wyjściowe kubity
examples.why: "qubit" appears two incompatible ways in one file: uninflected lowercase "qubit" in the first half ("wiele qubit") and capitalized, fully-declined "Qubity/Qubitów" here. Convention is lowercase declined "kubit/kubity".

examples.suggested: drugi z pewnością znajdzie się w stanie $\vert 1\rangle$
examples.why: "gwarantowanie" is not a valid Polish adverb here — a literal calque of "is guaranteed". Reads as machine output; a native writer would use "z pewnością" or "na pewno".

editor_note: This is an IBM Cloud shared-responsibilities reference (operations/compliance tables), not quantum pedagogy, so there are no physics terms (qubit/gate/observable) to vet; the relevant domain terminology is IT/ops, and it is handled correctly and consistently: "implantação" (deployment), "aplicação de patches" (patching), "determinação de problemas" (problem determination), "cargas de trabalho" (workloads), and "jobs" left in English per Qiskit convention. The prose reads as native technical Portuguese — the intro "Revise as seções a seguir para conhecer as responsabilidades específicas suas e da IBM" naturally smooths the English's compressed phrasing rather than calquing it, and the register is consistently informal "você"/imperative ("Defina", "Monitore", "Proteja", "Configure") with no formal slips. Meaning is faithful throughout: the critical negations ("A IBM não é responsável pelo backup...") and the "de ponta a ponta" (end-to-end) caveat are all preserved with no semantic drift. The only nit: "Rotate and update your API keys" is rendered "Alterne e atualize" — "Alterne" (alternate/toggle) understates key *rotation*; "Faça a rotação das chaves" would be sharper, but the meaning is not lost, so this stays a PASS.

examples.source: Rotate and update your API keys according to your security policy requirements.
examples.translation: Alterne e atualize suas chaves de API de acordo com os requisitos da sua política de segurança.
examples.suggested: Faça a rotação e atualize suas chaves de API de acordo com os requisitos da sua política de segurança.
examples.why: "Alterne" means alternate/toggle and slightly understates security key *rotation*; "fazer a rotação de chaves" is the conventional Portuguese rendering. Meaning is still recoverable, so this is a minor polish note, not a defect.

editor_note: This is fluent, native-sounding technical Brazilian Portuguese that teaches as clearly as the English — idiomatic choices like "equilíbrio delicado com a profundidade do circuito", "dê um passo atrás e pense em quantos parâmetros carregamos", and "Outra coisa bem diferente é..." read as authored, not machine-translated. Core domain terminology is correct and consistent: "qubit", "porta", "estado produto", "entrelaçamento", "espaço de Hilbert", "Hamiltoniano", "estado fundamental" (ground state), "transpilação". Semantics are faithful throughout — the normalization conditions, qubit-count claims, depth tradeoffs, and the Havlicek hardware-efficiency paragraph all match the source with no inverted conditions or dropped caveats — and informal "você" holds uniformly. Two isolated slips keep this from a clean PASS: (1) "feature/feature map" is rendered as "característica/mapeamento de características" in the first half (lines 19–361) but switches wholesale to the English "feature(s)/feature map(s)" from the "built-in feature maps" section onward (line 642 ff.), so the same concept is named two ways within one file; (2) one stray "portões de qubit único" at line 979 where every other gate reference in the file uses "portas". Neither misleads the reader.

examples.source: feature mapping ... 12 features ... a feature encoded on each qubit
examples.translation: mapeamento de características (early) vs "12 features" / "uma feature codificada em cada qubit" (later)
examples.suggested: Pick one rendering of feature/feature map and use it throughout (either consistently "característica"/"mapeamento de características", or consistently the English "feature"/"feature map")
examples.why: The first half glosses and translates the term as "característica"; the second half abandons that for the English "feature". Both are defensible individually, but the within-file inconsistency for the central concept of the lesson can confuse a learner into thinking two different things are meant.

examples.source: two-qubit error rates are much higher than single-qubit gate error rates
examples.translation: as taxas de erro de dois qubits são muito mais altas do que as de portões de qubit único
examples.suggested: ...do que as de portas de qubit único
examples.why: "portões" (large gates/portals) is the wrong sense of 'gate' here; the logic-gate sense "portas" is used everywhere else in the file (e.g. "portas parametrizadas", "porta Hadamard"). Isolated slip, mildly jarring to a technical reader.

editor_note: This reads like a Portuguese-speaking physicist wrote it, not like machine translation. The prose is fluent and idiomatic throughout — e.g. "as evidências de comportamento quantizado... estavam se acumulando", "Essa é a essência do experimento", "Chegamos bem longe em nossa análise", and the casual "spoiler: elas obedecem!" — and the informal "você" register holds consistently with no formal-address slips ("Fique à vontade", "Sinta-se à vontade", "Tente as perguntas abaixo"). Core domain terminology is correct and consistent: "medição", "momento angular", "vetor de estado", "autovalores/autovetores/autoestado", "porta Hadamard", "esfera de Bloch", "mudança de base", "observável", while terms the field keeps in English are correctly preserved (qubit, shots, backend, transpilar, Sampler, jobs, bins). Meaning is faithful paragraph by paragraph, including every caveat ("excluindo efeitos de ruído", "a menos de uma fase global") and the pedagogical intuition-building. The only blemishes are isolated and non-misleading: "inhomogeneous" is rendered "inomogêneo" in the Background section but "não-homogêneo" once at line 767, and "framework" is kept in English at line 571 but rendered "estrutura" at line 199 — both pairs are defensible Portuguese, just not internally uniform.

editor_note: This reads as genuine, well-edited Romanian technical prose, not raw MT: graph-theory terms are idiomatic ("vârfuri", "muchii direcționate", "seturi disjuncte"), domain terms are conventional and consistent ("harta de cuplare" for coupling map, "qubit-uri", "pasă/pase" for transpiler passes, "moștenească din" for inherit), and the informal "tu" register holds throughout ("Folosește", "să lucrezi", "Consultă") with no dumneavoastră slips. Meaning is faithful paragraph by paragraph — no semantic drift, no inverted conditions, and the layer-independence caveat ("operații care acționează pe seturi disjuncte de qubit-uri și pot fi astfel executate independent") is fully preserved. The only real defects are terminological-cosmetic: the word "gate" is rendered inconsistently as native "porți" (L15) but also as English "Gate-ul/Gate-urile" (L115, L127, L174), and at L139 it appears as the malformed hybrid plural "poartă-uri" (Romanian "poartă" + English "-uri" suffix) where standard "porți" is wanted. Neither misleads the reader; the prose teaches as clearly as the English.
